title: Size effects on generation-recombination noise
verified_location: Eqs. 1-3, drift-diffusion/Poisson equations, terminal current, Eqs. 20-21
terminal_boundary_condition: voltage-biased current noise; conduction plus displacement current
main_assumptions: unipolar; uniform coefficients; low-frequency analytical focus
main_result: finite size, diffusion, and Coulomb correlation modify GR current noise
limitations: not bipolar; ideal contacts; not HgCdTe-specific
novelty_implication: finite size plus Poisson plus diffusion is established

title: Current and voltage noise spectrum due to generation and recombination fluctuations in semiconductors
physical_model: finite semiconductor with carriers and traps
contact_boundary_conditions: finite-length carrier BC; exact form pending
main_result: full-frequency current/voltage GR spectra with Poisson and dynamic traps
limitations: full text unavailable in current audit

title: Generation-recombination noise in uniformly doped semiconductors with contacts of finite surface recombination velocities
physical_model: majority-carrier GR noise with finite contact velocity
main_result: field and contact velocity modify finite-sample GR spectrum
limitations: apparently monopolar; contact detailed balance unverified

title: Generation-recombination noise modelling in semiconductor devices through population or approximate equivalent current density fluctuations
physical_model: fundamental population versus equivalent current sources
recombination_model: direct and SRH with trap occupation
main_result: equivalent and monopolar sources fail when boundaries or minority carriers matter

title: Extension of the impedance field method to the noise analysis of a semiconductor junction: Analytical approach
authors: O. M. Bulashenko; G. Gomila; J. M. Rubi; V. A. Kochelap
covariance_basis: local correlations propagated by transfer fields
main_result: analytical terminal transfer from distributed noise sources
limitations: not a complete GR photoconductor model

title: Effects of blocking contacts on generation-recombination noise and responsivity in intrinsic photoconductors
physical_model: intrinsic photoconductor GR noise and responsivity
material: includes x approximately 0.2 HgCdTe at 77 K
contact_boundary_conditions: contact recombination velocity from ohmic to blocking
main_assumptions: blocking contacts alter responsivity and GR-noise rolloff
relevance_to_r06: HgCdTe contact-controlled GR noise is established

title: Theory of deep level trap effects on generation-recombination noise in HgCdTe photoconductors
authors: A. Evan Iverson; D. L. Smith
main_result: deep traps alter transport and add fluctuation contributions
novelty_implication: dynamic trap effects in HgCdTe GR noise are established

title: Two-dimensional modelling of HgCdTe photoconductive detectors
authors: E. P. G. Smith; C. A. Musca; L. Faraone
geometry: practical planar contacts versus ideal end contacts
main_result: planar geometry changes fields/carriers; 2D fit did not require fitted S
novelty_implication: 1D is an idealized regime map, not direct planar prediction

title: Investigation of the bulk and surface electronic properties of HgCdTe epitaxial layers using photoelectromagnetic
venue: G. Sarusi; A. Zemel; D. Eger; S. Ron; Y. Shapira
geometry: joint extraction of transport and recombination parameters
relevance_to_r06: mobility, diffusion length, lifetime, absorption, and surface velocity versus T; CdTe-capped S below 5000 cm/s at 77 K
novelty_implication: specimen-specific; passivated surface is not metal contact

title: Noise modeling of shunt resistance in HgCdTe photoconductor detectors
authors: R. K. Bhan; V. Gopal; R. S. Saxena; J. P. Singh
verified_location: abstract, introduction, model overview, experiment, conclusions
physical_model: bulk GR plus passivant-induced shunt noise
noise_primitives: bulk GR and lumped shunt contribution
main_result: bulk GR alone did not fit; surface shunts added noise
novelty_implication: departures from lumped GR are not unique evidence of spatial modes

title: Thermal Agitation of Electric Charge in Conductors
verified_location: APS record; convention translation by project
main_result: thermal terminal spectrum linked to dissipative impedance
novelty_implication: FDT recovery is validation, not novelty

title: Thermal Agitation of Electricity in Conductors
Statistics of the Recombinations of Holes and Electrons
authors: W. Shockley; W. T. Read Jr.
